Finance_Web_Scrapping

Name Meenakshi
Roll Number 8524227
Branch Artificial Intelligence And Machine Learning
College JMIETI
Company CodroidHub Pvt.Ltd
Instructor Devashish Kumar

Project Description

Finance Web Scraping is a Python-based project that extracts financial data from websites. The project collects information such as company names, stock prices, market capitalization, trading volume, daily highs and lows, percentage changes, and financial news. Python libraries like Requests and BeautifulSoup are used to retrieve and parse webpage content, while Pandas is used to organize the extracted data into a structured DataFrame. The cleaned data is then exported to a CSV file for further analysis, visualization,and reporting. This project demonstrates the practical application of web scraping techniques for collecting real-time financial data, which can be used for stock market analysis, investment research, financial trend analysis, portfolio management, and business intelligence.

📈 Finance Web Scraping - Imported Libraries

Library Purpose
requests Fetches HTML content from finance websites.
BeautifulSoup (bs4) Parses HTML and extracts financial information.
pandas Stores and organizes scraped financial data into DataFrames.
numpy Performs numerical calculations and handles missing values.
csv Exports the extracted financial data into CSV format.
matplotlib.pyplot Creates charts and graphs for financial analysis.

Purpose:

These libraries are used to scrape financial information such as company names, stock prices, market capitalization, trading volume, price changes, and financial news. The extracted data is cleaned, organized using Pandas, and exported into CSV format for financial analysis, visualization, investment research, and market trend analysis.

Step 1: Send Request¶

In [1]:
import requests

Step 2: Parse HTML¶

In [2]:
url ='https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/currencies/'
response = requests.get(url)
In [3]:
response.status_code
headers = {
    "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/137.0.0.0 Safari/537.36",
    "Accept": "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8",
    "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.9",
    "Connection": "keep-alive",
    "Upgrade-Insecure-Requests": "1",
    "Referer": "https://www.google.com/"
}

response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)

print(response.status_code)

200
In [4]:
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
soup= BeautifulSoup(response.text,'html.parser')
soup.title
Out[4]:
<title>Forex Currencies: live prices, changes, trading volume and daily charts - Yahoo Finance</title>
In [5]:
soup
Out[5]:

Please try again.","YODLEE_BANK_ERROR":"Sorry, we couldn’t connect your bank account","YODLEE_TIMEOUT":"Your session has timed out. Please sign in again","YODLEE_BANK_LOADING":"Loading","MIN_AMOUNT":"You can buy minimum of $1.00","IMPORTANT_DATE":"Important dates","CREATE_PORTFOLIO_MANUALLY":"Create a portfolio manually"}}); function initStreamer(){ window.finWebCore.addAsset('streamer',{async:true}); } if(document.readyState === 'interactive' || document.readyState === 'complete'){ initStreamer(); }else{window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', initStreamer);}}

Step 3: Print Length¶

In [7]:
rows = soup.find_all("tr")
print(len(rows))

25

Step 4: Extracting Data From Finance¶

In [8]:
currencies_data = []

for row in rows[1:]:   # header row skip
    cols = row.find_all("td")

    if len(cols) > 0:
        currency = cols[0].get_text(strip=True)
        price = cols[1].get_text(strip=True)
        change = cols[2].get_text(strip=True)
        percent_change = cols[3].get_text(strip=True)

        currencies_data.append({
            "Currency": currency,
            "Price": price,
            "Change": change,
            "% Change": percent_change
        })

Step 5: Data Storage And Data Creation¶

In [9]:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame(currencies_data)
df

Observation

  1. Successfully established a connection with the finance website and retrieved financial data.
  2. Financial details such as Company Name, Stock Price, Market Capitalization, Trading Volume, Daily High, Daily Low, and Price Change were extracted accurately.
  3. The Requests library was used to fetch webpage content, while BeautifulSoup was used to parse the HTML and extract the required information.
  4. The extracted data was cleaned, processed, and organized into a Pandas DataFrame for efficient analysis.
  5. Missing values and duplicate records were identified and handled to improve the quality of the dataset.
  6. The final dataset was successfully exported to CSV format for further analysis, reporting, and visualization.
  7. The collected financial data provides valuable insights into stock market performance, company trends, and investment opportunities.

Conclusion

  1. The Finance Web Scraping project was successfully completed using Python and web scraping techniques.
  2. Financial information such as Company Name, Stock Price, Market Capitalization, Trading Volume, Daily High, Daily Low, and Price Change was extracted accurately.
  3. The Requests and BeautifulSoup libraries were used to retrieve and parse webpage content efficiently.
  4. The extracted data was cleaned, processed, and organized into a Pandas DataFrame for easy analysis and storage.
  5. The final dataset was exported to CSV format, making it suitable for visualization, reporting, and further financial analysis.
  6. The collected data can be used for stock market analysis, investment research, financial trend analysis, portfolio management, and business intelligence.
  7. This project demonstrates the practical application of web scraping techniques for collecting real-time financial data and transforming it into meaningful insights for decision-making.
